articles.title: Heatwave: Odisha Government may extend Summer Vacation - prameyanews.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, June 4: The Odisha government may extend the ongoing summer vacation in schools depending on the prevailing heatwave conditions in the state, School and Mass Education Minister Nityananda Gond said.
The minister stated that any decision regarding the extension of holidays will be taken based on the situation at that time. “If the heat does not subside, the summer vacation may be extended. The safety of students remains the top priority of the department,” he said.
Notably, in view of the intense heatwave conditions across the state, the government had earlier declared summer holidays for schools from April 27. The decision was taken to ensure the safety of students and was approved by Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi following a proposal from the School and Mass Education Department.

articles.title: Summer vacation likely to be extended in Odisha schools amid prevailing heatwave - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
The Odisha government is likely to extend the summer vacation in schools across the state in view of the prevailing heatwave condition.
As per reports, the state government is likely to take a decision in this regard after reviewing the situation.
Nityananda Gond, School and Mass Education Minister indicated that the government is considering the possibility of extending the ongoing summer break. The decision, however, will depend on the weather conditions and the intensity of the heat across the state in the coming days.
Decision To Depend On Heatwave Situation
According to the minister, the decision will be taken after closely monitoring the heatwave conditions before taking a final call on the reopening of schools.
“The decision will be taken after assessing the prevailing heatwave conditions across the state. The health and safety of students remain the government's top priority, and a decision on extending the summer vacation will be made accordingly,” he said.
While monsoon is expected to reach the Odisha coast by mid-June, reports suggest that weather patterns could be influenced by the El Niño effect, potentially leading to a rise in temperatures and heatwave conditions.
Schools Earlier Scheduled To Reopen On June 22
The state government had earlier announced that schools would reopen on June 22 following the summer vacation.
However, with concerns over the prevailing heat conditions, the possibility of extending the holiday period is now under consideration.
An official announcement on the matter is expected once the government completes its assessment of the heatwave conditions.

articles.title: Summer vacation for schools likely to extend in view of prevailing heatwave - Kalinga TV
articles.full_text:
Summer vacation for schools likely to extend in view of prevailing heatwave
Odisha government likely to extend summer vacation for schools in the entire state, informed Minister of School and Mass Education Nityananda Gond
Bhubaneswar: Due to prevailing heatwave conditions, the Odisha government likely to extend summer vacation for schools in the entire state, informed Minister of School and Mass Education Nityananda Gond.
“Time will tell whether the summer vacation will be extended or not. A decision will be taken at that time based on the situation,” minister said.
“If the heat does not subside, the holidays may be extended. Student safety is our top priority,” he added.
The state government had announced an early summer vacation for all schools from April 27 due to the severe heatwave conditions across the state. The vacation was originally scheduled to end on June 17. However, officials have indicated that the duration may be revised depending on the intensity of the heat.
